enonce.texte:
Read the text below about household garbage disposal and answer the questions that follow.

Text: Proper Waste Disposal

Garbage is a big problem in many cities. Every day, families throw away plastic bottles, food waste, and paper. Some people burn their garbage, which causes pollution. Others throw it in rivers, which harms fish and other animals. The best way to dispose of garbage is to sort it. Organic waste can be used as compost for plants. Plastic and glass can be recycled. Paper can be reused. Governments provide garbage bins and collection services to help communities stay clean.
images: A simple illustration showing a family sorting garbage into different bins: green for organic waste, blue for plastic and glass, and yellow for paper.

questions.texte: Identify the different types of waste mentioned in the text.
questions.reponse: The types of waste mentioned are: plastic bottles, food waste, paper, organic waste, plastic and glass.

questions.texte: Explain why burning garbage is harmful.
questions.reponse: Burning garbage is harmful because it causes pollution and can release toxic substances into the air, which can affect people's health and the environment.

questions.texte: Analyse the best way to dispose of garbage according to the text.
questions.reponse: According to the text, the best way to dispose of garbage is to sort it. Organic waste can be used as compost, plastic and glass can be recycled, and paper can be reused. This reduces pollution and helps protect the environment.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct interrogative pronoun: who, what, which.

questions.texte: _______ is responsible for collecting the garbage in your neighbourhood?
_______ type of waste can be recycled?
_______ of these bins is for organic waste?
_______ did you give the plastic bottles to?
_______ is the best way to dispose of electronic waste?
_______ one of these bags contains glass?

intitule: Writing Task: Advice on Garbage Disposal

enonce.texte: Your community has a problem with garbage disposal. Write a short paragraph (5-7 sentences) giving advice to your neighbours on how to dispose of garbage properly. Use at least two interrogative pronouns (who, what, which) in your advice.
images: A picture of a dirty street with garbage scattered around, showing the consequences of poor waste disposal.

questions.texte: Write your paragraph of advice here.
questions.reponse: Example answer: "Dear neighbours, garbage disposal is a serious problem in our community. Who is responsible for keeping our street clean? We all are! What can we do to improve the situation? First, we should separate our waste into different bins. Organic waste can be used for composting. Plastic bottles and glass should be put in recycling bins. Which bin is for paper? The yellow one! Remember, a clean environment is good for everyone's health. Let's work together to keep our community clean."

enonce.texte:
Read the newspaper article below and answer the questions.

Article: City Council Tackles Garbage Problem

Last week, the city council announced a new plan to manage garbage disposal. The plan includes providing more garbage bins in residential areas. The council will also organize weekly collection services. Residents are encouraged to sort their waste into three categories: organic, recyclable, and non-recyclable. Who will benefit from this plan? Everyone! The council says that proper waste management will reduce pollution and create a healthier environment. Which neighbourhoods will receive the new bins first? The areas with the most garbage problems. What can residents do to help? They can separate their waste and avoid burning garbage.
images: A city council meeting with officials discussing waste management plans.

questions.texte: Identify the three categories of waste mentioned in the article.
questions.reponse: The three categories are: organic, recyclable, and non-recyclable waste.

questions.texte: Explain how the city council plans to help residents with garbage disposal.
questions.reponse: The city council plans to provide more garbage bins in residential areas and organize weekly collection services. They also encourage residents to sort their waste.

questions.texte: Analyse the benefits of proper waste management according to the article.
questions.reponse: According to the article, proper waste management will reduce pollution and create a healthier environment. This benefits everyone in the community.

intitule: Filling Out a Form on Garbage Disposal

enonce.texte:
Your school is organizing a clean-up campaign. Fill out the form below with the correct information about your household garbage disposal habits.

Household Garbage Disposal Survey

Name: _________________________

Class: _________________________

1. What type of garbage does your household produce the most?
☐ Plastic ☐ Food waste ☐ Paper ☐ Glass

2. Who is responsible for taking out the garbage in your home?
__________________________________

3. Which recycling method do you use?
☐ Composting ☐ Recycling bins ☐ Burning ☐ Throwing away

4. How often is garbage collected in your area?
☐ Daily ☐ Weekly ☐ Monthly ☐ Never
images: An illustration of a clean neighbourhood with garbage bins and a recycling truck.

questions.texte: Fill out the form based on your household garbage disposal habits.
questions.reponse: Example answer: Name: John Doe; Class: Form 3A; 1. Plastic; 2. My mother; 3. Recycling bins; 4. Weekly.

intitule: Writing a Notice on Garbage Disposal

enonce.texte:
As the class representative, you are asked to write a notice to inform students about a new garbage disposal policy at school. Write a notice of about 80 words.

Your notice should include:

  • A clear title
  • At least two interrogative pronouns (who, what, which)
  • Instructions on how to dispose of garbage properly
images: A clean school environment with clearly labeled garbage bins.

questions.texte: Write the notice for your classmates.
questions.reponse: Example answer: NOTICE: NEW GARBAGE DISPOSAL POLICY Dear students, Please be informed that the school has introduced a new waste management policy. Who is responsible for keeping our school clean? We all are! What should we do? Separate your waste into three bins: green for organic waste, blue for plastic, and yellow for paper. Which bin should you use for food leftovers? The green one! Remember, a clean school is a healthy school. Let's all do our part! Thank you. Class Representative
